justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical, jsonwebtoken CVEs flagged as Critical with specific CVE numbers, 5/5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, crypto-random-string, left-pad, moment), safe_to_deploy is false, and severity ratings are well-differentiated (Critical for stripe/jsonwebtoken/crypto-random-string, Important for moment/left-pad, plus suggestions).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with clear explanation of payment corruption risk
jsonwebtoken flagged with specific CVE numbers (CVE-2022-23529, 23539, 23540, 23541) and concrete exploitation scenario
crypto-random-string ESM/CJS incompatibility identified with exact error name (ERR_REQUIRE_ESM) and fix alternatives
left-pad supply chain risk noted with historical context and native replacement (padStart)
moment.js deprecation correctly flagged at Important level with alternatives suggested
Bonus finding: pg version range flagged as excessively broad — goes beyond expected risks
Actionable fix suggestions with time estimates for each critical finding
Low confidence areas honestly note the CJS assumption for crypto-random-string

crypto-random-string rated Critical rather than Important — the rubric expected Important since it causes a runtime error but is detectable in testing; however this is a minor severity disagreement and the identification itself is correct
